title: Evaluating protein intake-induced feeding inhibition in Drosophila
author: Wang, Wang, et al.

steps.title: Data analysis
steps.instruction: This section describes statistical analysis of PIFI effect and excretion level of flies.36.Following sample processing, export the data recorded by the absorbance plate reader.37.Average the reading results of the 3 loading repeats of PBS as “Average PBS”.38.To measure Food Consumption (FC), average the readings of the 3 repeats from the same cryogenic vial of agar or tryptone pre-feeding groups and subtract the Average PBS, recorded as “FCAgar(i)” or “FCTryptone(i)”.FCAgar(i) = (Repeat 1+Repeat 2+Repeat 3)/3–Average PBSFCTryptone(i) = (Repeat 1+Repeat 2+Repeat 3)/3–Average PBS39.To quantify the food consumption of n trails of PIFI experiments, average the FCAgar(i) of the maternal control groups as “Average (FCMaternal Control)”.Average(FCMaternalControl)=(∑i=1nFCAgar(i))n40.To obtain the Relative Food Consumption (RFC), normalize each group to the maternal control.RFCAgar(i)=FCAgar(i)Average(FCMaternalControl)i=1…nRFCTryptone(i)=FCTryptone(i)Average(FCMaternalControl)i=1…n41.To obtain the Suppression Index (SI) for each genotype, calculate the Average(RFCAgar) and subtract it from the RFCTryptone(i). The difference is divided by Average(RFCAgar), and the ratio is marked as the Suppression Index of Tryptone (SIT).Average(RFCAgar)=(∑i=1nRFCAgar(i))nSIT(i)=RFCTryptone(i)−Average(RFCAgar)Average(RFCAgar)i=1…n42.To obtain RFC for the tryptone feeding test, average the FCTryptone(i) of the maternal control groups as “Average(FCMaternal Control)”.Average(FCMaternalControl)=(∑i=1nFCTryptone(i))nRFCTryptone(i)=FCTryptone(i)Average(FCMaternalControl)i=1…n43.To obtain Relative Excretion (REX) for the Excretion test, adjust the value by dividing Nflies and multiplying by 30 for each repeat. Calculate the excretion level as ExcretionAgar(i) or ExcretionTryptone(i) following the steps described in steps 37 and 38. Normalize each group to the food consumption of maternal control.REXAgar(i)=ExcretionAgar(i)Average(FCMaternalControl)i=1…nREXTryptonei=ExcretionTryptoneiAverageFCMaternalControli=1…n44.Calculate the Excretion-to-Retention Ratio (ERR) by dividing ExcretionAgar(i) and ExcretionTryptone(i) with the averaged FCAgar and FCTryptone, respectively.ERRAgar(i)=ExcretionAgar(i)Average(FCAgar)i=1…nERRTryptone(i)=ExcretionTryptone(i)Average(FCTryptone)i=1…n45.Analyze all data using GraphPad Prism and plot as mean ± standard error of mean (S.E.M.). Use Student’s t test to compare two groups, and One-way ANOVA followed by Dunnett’s multiple comparisons test for comparing among groups. Use Two-way ANOVA to determine the interaction between two factors. ∗, p < 0.05; ∗∗, p < 0.01; ∗∗∗, p < 0.001. See also Table S2.
